titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde, 8 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941) to Thomas Henry Grattan Esmonde (1862-1935). Magill was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921. Esmonde was a landowner and politician. The letter acknowledges Esmonde's recommendation that John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943) be appointed chief crown solicitor and that O'Connell's claims will be considered before any decision is made.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/7

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 8th April, 1916. Dear Sir Thomas Esmonde, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of 7th instant, recommending
Sir John R. O'Connell for appointment as Chief Crown
Solicitor for Ireland, and to say that his claims will
be fully considered before an appointment is made. Yours faithfully,
(Sd.) A.P.M.
Under Secretary
A.P.M.
8/4/16
Sir Thomas H. Grattan Esmonde, Bart., M.P., Ballynastragh, Inch, Co.Wexford
